meaning: vt.使沮丧,使消沉,压下,压低,使不活泼,使萧条v.压下
examples: Subsidies allow growers to undercut competitors and depress world prices.
The atmosphere began to depress me.
Runaway inflation will depress the economy.
Now that's a thought to depress you if you're on the wrong side of 40.
Wall Street analysts don't think that the decreased float will depress Internet stock prices.
